heroDesc: Additive manufacturing for complex metal structures
bodyTitle: Metal 3D Printing for internal channels, lightweight structures and low-volume metal parts
bodyDesc: Metal 3D printing is useful for complex internal channels, lattice lightweighting, conformal cooling and low-volume high-value metal parts that are difficult to machine subtractively. Common powder-bed fusion parts usually require support removal, heat treatment, blasting and CNC post-machining on critical mating surfaces.

panels.title: Design Value
panels.subtitle: Internal Channels
panels.body: Good for channels, lattice and integrated structures hard to reach by machining.

panels.title: Quality Focus
panels.subtitle: Support / Distortion
panels.body: Overhangs, thermal stress and powder removal affect delivery quality.

panels.title: Post-Processing
panels.subtitle: Heat Treat / CNC
panels.body: Mating faces, holes and threads are usually post-machined.

tableRows: Depends on machine, material and throughput
Critical dimensions should be CNC machined
Depends on material and support strategy
Threads and sealing faces should be machined

steps.title: Additive manufacturability review
steps.body: Check walls, channels, support, powder removal and machining allowance.

steps.title: Orientation and support design
steps.body: Control thermal distortion, surface direction and support-removal access.

steps.title: Printing and heat treatment
steps.body: Build, depowder, remove support and stress relieve as needed.

steps.title: Post-machining and inspection
steps.body: Finish mating faces, holes, threads and sealing faces.

asideCta.title: Need a Manufacturing Plan?
asideCta.body: After drawings are submitted, engineers can review geometry, material and volume to suggest a suitable process route.

related.title: Related Process Recommendations
related.desc: Depending on geometry, material and delivery stage, other processes can be combined into a complete manufacturing flow.

items.title: CNC Machining
items.body: Post-machines critical surfaces on printed parts.

items.title: Complex Part Machining
items.body: Combines process planning for difficult geometry.

faq.title: Metal 3D Printing FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Metal 3D Printing suitable for?
items.a: Metal 3D Printing is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Metal 3D Printing quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Metal 3D Printing?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
